title: hedysarum alpinum subsp. laxiflorum
intro: Hedysarum alpinum subsp. laxiflorum, also known as the alpine sweet pea or mountain hedysarum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to promote digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, making it a popular herbal remedy in some parts of the world. In traditional herbal preparations, Hedysarum alpinum subsp. laxiflorum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be made into a salve or ointment for topical application.
